title: Duellum Veritatis claim/evidence matrix
description: Maps each (sub)claim to the evidence that bears on it, the evidence's diagnosticity and direction, the claim's scope, and the admission ladder (accepted / rejected / quarantined). Forces the reader to see which sub-claim was killed, which survived, and which is only speculation.

claim_id.$comment: Stable id for this (sub)claim, unique within the matrix. The arbiter_report references these ids so its supports/does-not-support cannot float free of the evidence ledger.

evidence_id.$comment: Stable id for this row's evidence, unique within the matrix.

evidence.$comment: Pointer or description; 'not tested' is a legitimate value.

scope.$comment: The exact regime/model the row is valid within (e.g. 'toy chiral-helix vs isotropic random only').

evidence_status.$comment: Admission ladder: was this evidence admitted, rejected, or quarantined.

claim_status.$comment: Disposition of the claim given admitted evidence. 'unsupported' = claim made but not backed (overclaim risk).
